briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the climate briefing for April 23, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Severity of the Climate Crisis and Call for Immediate Action
sections.content: News of a 22% decline in emperor penguin populations over the past five years [2, 46] highlights the urgency of penguin protection on 'World Penguin Day' [43] and once again reminds us of the severe impact of climate change on ecosystems. The phenomenon of earlier annual pollen dispersal [15, 22] is also emerging as visible evidence of climate change. Reports that weather disasters have disrupted 94 elections worldwide [25] and that 99% of U.S. cities have warmed since 1970 [194, 204] demonstrate the widespread impact of climate change across society. Scientists are predicting wildfire risks for species survival amidst climate change [191], and the Secretary-General of the World Meteorological Organization (WMO) has urged stronger international action on climate risks [209]. In particular, warnings about the potential collapse of critical Atlantic systems [218] underscore the urgency of an immediate global response.

sections.title: 🌍 Dual Nature of International Cooperation and Policy Trends
sections.content: Our Ministry of Foreign Affairs is actively participating in international climate change discussions by dispatching a Climate Change Ambassador to the 'Petersberg Climate Dialogue,' a high-level consultative body on climate change [1, 4, 17, 38]. Furthermore, Yeosu City is making all-out efforts to host the 33rd UN Climate Change Conference of the Parties (COP33) in 2028 [13, 34, 52], positioning itself as an optimal location for achieving carbon neutrality. However, France's announcement that the G7 excluded climate change from its Paris agenda [187] and reports that the COP31 presidency prioritizes the global economy despite worsening climate impacts [212] suggest difficulties in international cooperation. In Florida, USA, a movement to sign legislation halting local climate change efforts has also been observed [190], raising concerns about policy setbacks. Amidst this situation, Colombia is set to host an international 'fossil-fuel-free' conference to complement a diluted climate agreement following the US withdrawal [185], drawing attention to new forms of international cooperative efforts.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Change Response and Efforts to Expand Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Domestically, various carbon neutrality initiatives were actively carried out across the country to mark 'Earth Day' and 'Climate Change Week' [10, 14, 26, 27, 29, 33, 35, 37, 39, 40, 42, 45, 60, 63, 67, 72, 73, 76, 93, 101, 103, 107, 113, 115]. Notably, Yangpyeong County achieved a reduction of 2.51 tons of greenhouse gases through a 10-minute lights-out campaign [24], and Uiryeong County encouraged daily carbon neutrality practices by operating a 'Vegetarian Meal Day' [5, 31]. Local governments and institutions are also focusing on environmental education and capacity building; Anseong City conducted carbon neutrality training for public officials [48, 49, 50, 69, 86, 116, 118], and Siheung City established a 2030 environmental education roadmap to foster 'climate citizens' [54, 55, 56, 64, 100]. Furthermore, discussions are underway on climate change response measures utilizing artificial intelligence (AI) [8, 20], and the APEC Climate Center is contributing to strengthening international climate crisis response capabilities by conducting training on climate prediction systems with Pacific island nations [21, 44]. Specific carbon neutrality technologies and business model developments, such as reusable container circulation projects [87], eco-friendly livestock systems [85], and golf course energy models utilizing waste heat from hydrogen fuel cells [68, 114], are also being actively pursued.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Challenges
sections.content: Korea is accelerating efforts to expand renewable energy. Korea Western Power is speeding up its energy transition by significantly expanding its renewable energy business division and reinforcing personnel to achieve its target of 13.9 GW of renewable energy by 2040 [122, 128, 129, 131, 138, 142, 146, 147, 149, 151, 153, 157, 159, 175, 176, 177, 179]. Regional expansion of renewable energy supply is also active, with schools in the Gwangju area transforming into renewable energy production bases by installing solar power facilities [134, 165, 169, 174, 183], and Chungnam Province aiming for rural revitalization through the creation of 'Sunlight Income Villages' [167]. Gyeongnam Energy is accelerating the energy transition by expanding cooperation with Tongyeong City on city gas and new and renewable energy [126, 135, 148, 152, 154, 158, 161, 172, 180], and Jeollanam-do is expanding its capabilities overseas by promoting the creation of renewable energy-based 'Energy Self-Sufficient Villages' in Senegal, Africa [139, 155]. However, challenges in the process of renewable energy expansion also exist, with civil society demanding that the government specify a 100 GW renewable energy target [137, 166, 171, 178, 182], and discussions on nuclear power re-evaluation emerging in Europe due to renewable energy bottlenecks [181]. KAIST researchers warned that Korea must reduce its reliance on LNG and accelerate the qualitative transition to renewable energy [184], emphasizing the need for in-depth strategy development for a sustainable energy transition.

briefing.closing: As confirmed in today's briefing, climate change has already deeply permeated our lives and ecosystems. Immediate response, international cooperation, and practical efforts for carbon neutrality and renewable energy transition, both domestically and internationally, are urgently needed. This concludes today's climate briefing.

summary.phenomenon: Bluefield Solar has projected a modest impact on its Net Asset Value (NAV) following the abolition of the carbon tax, a consequence of evolving energy policy. Specific data regarding figures, regions, or timelines were not detailed in the report.
summary.cause: The primary cause is attributed to the repeal of the carbon tax, which resulted from shifts in energy policy.
summary.outlook: A minor adverse effect on Bluefield Solar's Net Asset Value is anticipated.
